STC is an NGO founded in the UK in 1919 to improve children's lives globally.
ER is attempting to convince EE to make a donation to STC.
Your task is to determine the real intention of the last utterance based on the conversation.

ER: I have donated to organization that works on some disease through MTurk, like Malaria
EE: I'm grasping at straws now, 10 turns each is a monumental task just like fighting childhood cancer (not really) have you ever donated to charities for childhood cancer?
ER: yes I did it before. Actually it breaks my heart to see children in pain.
ER: I think It is good to know donating through this task won't hurt your reward
EE: Childrens charities are important to increase awareness for diseases and disabilities. I cant even imagine what these poor families go through.
EE: McDonalds does a charity that helps house families near hospitals so they can be together during a hospital stay. Ronald McDonald House is the name I think.
ER: I will look for it for sure.
ER: I like it when part of my purchase will be donated.
ER: Thank you for all the information
EE: It's been a pleasure chatting with you about children charities. I think this is the last chat exchange. Have a good day!

They have been assigned the roles of persuader (ER) and persuadee (EE), and they are discussing Save the Children (STC), a charitable organization.
STC is an NGO founded in the UK in 1919 to improve children's lives globally.
ER is attempting to convince EE to make a donation to STC.
Your task is to determine the real intention of the last utterance based on the conversation.

ER: Do you think you would be willing to make a small donation to Save the Children today?
EE: I most likely will. I would like to read some more information, probably on their website, then donate from there.
EE: How do they help children in other parts of the world?
ER: Well, I know that the research team for this task can take a portion of our payment and send it directly to the Save the Children.
ER: It's quick and easy, and even thought it may not seem like a lot of money, I think that every penny counts. I believe we can contribute any portion of our payment: $0 - 0.30.
EE: That would be alright.
EE: Yes, I would like to donate at least one dollar to help Save the Children.
ER: Oh great! I don't know if there is a maximum we can contribute, but regardless, I think your support would be really appreciated.
EE: I've heard on the news that many children around the world are with their families trying to escape wars.
EE: I'm sure Save the Children helps them?
